evals.expected_output: A read-only README audit with section or card findings, strengths, and validation risks.
expected_behavior: Reads AGENTS.md and README.md before reporting findings.
Runs or requests git status before any edit.
Produces a read-only section or card audit.
Does not modify files in audit mode.
assertions: Mentions that audit mode is read-only.
Includes at least one exact README section or card target.
Does not propose file edits as already completed.

evals.expected_output: A source-verified method-card plan or patch that satisfies the README Card Contract.
expected_behavior: Verifies method-specific official docs or primary sources before editing.
Applies the full README Card Contract including paste-zone table rules.
Uses the canonical Fill these in pointer: Match the **Paste zones** table above; paste `none` for optional zones you omit.
Updates badge/heading config in scripts/update_readme_badges.py when adding recipes.
Includes safety, cost, latency, failure-mode, caveat, and eval notes.
Avoids visible long chain-of-thought as a default template instruction.
assertions: Includes clickable source URLs or states that source verification is missing.
Includes an eval requirement.
Separates trusted context from untrusted input in any template.

evals.expected_output: A focused revision that preserves useful anchors and improves the existing ReAct method card.
expected_behavior: Reads the existing ReAct card before proposing changes.
Preserves useful anchors unless there is a documented reason to change them.
Checks source quality and template hygiene.
Runs validation after editing.
assertions: References the existing card by name.
Does not remove method-card contract fields.
Does not make public long chain-of-thought the default.

evals.prompt: Refresh README provider and model guidance using the latest official docs.
evals.expected_output: A provider guidance refresh grounded in official docs, with stale or unverifiable claims flagged.
expected_behavior: Activates readme-catalog-steward behavior.
Uses official provider docs before practitioner or community sources.
Records URLs and access dates for current claims.
Flags unverified or stale provider/model claims instead of repeating them.
assertions: Cites official provider docs.
Labels unverifiable model claims as such.
Avoids unsupported latest-model language.

evals.expected_output: A README safety audit covering prompt injection, tools, RAG trust boundaries, refusal, abstention, and high-stakes review.
expected_behavior: Reads relevant safety and eval README sections.
Uses the card contract and source policy where needed.
Prioritizes visible safety warnings and validation requirements.
Does not hide critical safety guidance only inside collapses.
assertions: Mentions prompt injection.
Mentions high-stakes human review boundaries.
Mentions structured output or parser validation when relevant.

evals.expected_output: A README presentation audit or patch that improves GFM navigation/readability without unsupported badge claims.
expected_behavior: Checks badges, TOC, alerts, tables, Mermaid, generated marker blocks, lane chips, icon-only recipe headings, and accessibility.
Preserves truthful badge semantics and marker boundaries.
Runs markdown and link validation after editing.
assertions: Does not add CI, package, license, release, coverage, or provider badges without support.
Mentions icon-only recipe heading badges or generated badge markers.
Mentions validation commands.

evals.expected_output: A badge-surface audit or regeneration plan covering hero badges, shortcuts, lanes, lane chips, job map, and 48 recipe heading icons.
expected_behavior: Reads references/badge-surfaces.md or equivalent badge contract.
Uses scripts/update_readme_badges.py for generated blocks; does not hand-edit ShieldCN URLs.
Treats recipe heading badges as icon-only with alt/title tooltips.
Runs badge --check and curl-verifies changed URLs when applicable.
assertions: Mentions update_readme_badges.py or badge drift check.
Distinguishes icon-only heading badges from worded lane chips.
Does not propose Filled example walkthrough blocks.

evals.expected_output: A README eval and contribution checklist improvement focused on regression sets and prompt versioning.
expected_behavior: Reviews eval flywheel guidance before editing.
Adds or improves concrete regression-set requirements.
Keeps eval advice tied to README method cards.
assertions: Mentions representative failure cases.
Mentions prompt/model/retrieval/tool version metadata.
Mentions parser-invalid or refusal cases where relevant.

evals.expected_output: Runs or cites the AGENTS validation block and reports exact failures without editing files.
expected_behavior: Reads AGENTS.md Validation section or lists equivalent commands.
Runs or instructs recipe check, paste-zone audit, unittest discover, badge --check, markdown lint, and link check.
Reports failures verbatim with command output or error codes.
Does not edit README, scripts, skill files, or workflow.
assertions: Mentions validate mode or AGENTS validation block.
Cites at least two concrete validation commands from the block.
Does not claim files were edited or fixes applied.

evals.expected_output: A mode menu with examples and the initial repository reads required before ambiguous work.
expected_behavior: Shows available modes in SKILL.md dispatch-table order.
Lists validate as a distinct mode for running the AGENTS validation block.
Reads or instructs the agent to read AGENTS.md, README.md, and git status.
Asks only if intent remains ambiguous.
assertions: Lists modes in dispatch-table order: audit, refresh-sources, add-card, revise-card, safety-pass, gfm-pass, badge-pass, eval-pass, validate.
Mentions validate as a distinct validation mode separate from eval-pass.
Does not start a full README rewrite without user intent.

evals.prompt: Run this prompt and show me the answer: summarize the pasted article.
evals.expected_output: A redirection away from README stewardship toward a normal prompt-running or summarization workflow.
expected_behavior: Does not activate README stewardship behavior.
Does not edit README.md.
Redirects to a normal prompt-running or summarization workflow.
assertions: States that README stewardship is not the right workflow.
Does not modify files.

evals.prompt: Create an MCP server for this repository.
evals.expected_output: A redirection away from README stewardship toward the appropriate MCP or engineering workflow.
expected_behavior: Does not use readme-catalog-steward as the primary workflow.
States that the skill is not for MCP server creation.
Redirects to the appropriate MCP or engineering workflow.
assertions: Contains a NOT-for-MCP boundary.
Does not create skill or README edits as part of this request.

evals.expected_output: A concise clarification request for the missing method name.
expected_behavior: Detects that add-card requires a method target.
Asks for the missing method name.
Does not edit README.md.
assertions: Mentions the missing method target.
Does not fabricate a method.
